chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: If you have read about set, then you must have read the set theory  In which it is said that set is a collection of well-defined objects  Now everything happens in Python  And if you want a data type in Python in which repeated entries cannot come  Then you can make a set

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: I will give you an example, a very good example  If you want to collect the list of people whose name starts with S  And suppose you are going to ask different people their names  So someone tells you Shruti, someone tells you Samar, someone tells you Shubham  It is possible that one of your friends tells you Shubham again

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So you don't want to add it to the list  Because if 100 people told Shubham, then 100 Shubham will be in your list  So you want that whatever items you are maintaining in the list should not be repeated  For this we can make a set  We will see what are the methods of set and why is it used

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started  Many of you must have read in mathematics that set is a collection of well-defined objects  Well, sets are of the same kind in Python  Now what does set mean in Python?  Why did set come in Python?

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Sometimes we have to collect some values that we want to not repeat  Suppose I want to ask you the employee ID of those people who have already received the gift  Now you are told that you report those who have already received the gift  Suppose a company wants to give Thanksgiving gift to all employees once  And you have to ensure that no one takes more than once

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So every employee is told to submit the ID of the person who has received the gift  If we solve this problem with a list, then that list will be very big and it will have repeated values  It is possible that one employee takes the name of 5 employees  And the other employee takes the name of exactly those 5 employees  Or take the name of 3 employees out of those 5

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So what will happen if this happens?  The list will be very big and we will need more memory  We don't want this  That's why set data type has come in Python  So how do we make a set?

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: If I write s is equal to curly bracket  Suppose I write 2, 4, 2 and 6  And if I print this  You will see that s is being repeated twice  But the set will take it only once

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So 2, 4, 2, 6 will actually become 2, 4, 6  As you can see  So set doesn't take repeated values  So this is a set of well-defined objects  Set is a collection of well-defined objects

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: And if I talk about set here  There are many methods for it  You can add values to it  You can do everything that you do with a list  In a very similar fashion

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Except the fact that values cannot be repeated  So sets are unordered collection of data items  Here the word unordered is very important  Because it doesn't maintain order  You can never expect that

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: First 2 comes, then 4 comes, then 6 comes  There is no guarantee to maintain this order  You have to remember this  Set items are separated by commas and enclosed within curly brackets  As I have done here

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: The values of it are kept comma separated  And instead of square brackets  Which we use in our list  Or parentheses which we use in tuple  Here we use curly brackets

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Something like this  And this tells the Python interpreter  Because we are talking about set  Sets are unchangeable  Meaning you cannot change items of the set once created

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Sets do not contain duplicate items  Now understand this  Sets are unchangeable means  If I want to keep 8 where 2 is  Then I can't do this

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Once 2 goes inside the set  You can consider it as a bag  A very big bag  In which there are many pearls  If you put a pearl

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Then it gets mixed in the bag  You can't find it and change it  You can't replace it  Understand it like this  Now here it is not necessary

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Like in the list  As we saw in the list  Different data types can be added  It can be added here also  So you see

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Karla 19, false 5.9  19 will be added only once  Now 19 can be added in the starting  19 can be added in the end  There is no guarantee of maintaining the order of Python

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So you should know this  You see here its output is this  But in my case, this is the output  So there is no guarantee of order  You can see

chunks.text: When I run this program  So this must have come as the output  That's why I wrote  But you see  Output is here

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: I am getting to see this  So there is no guarantee of order  You will get to see anything anywhere  Order  You can't say that

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: I got the order maintained  There is no guarantee  Okay  Here we see that the items of set  Occur in random order

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: And hence they cannot be accessed using index numbers  You can't do this  Info 0, info 1, info 2  No  They don't maintain the order

chunks.text: Okay  Also, the sets don't allow duplicate values  We have seen this  Okay  Now this quick quiz may blow your mind
